{"data":{"isTableReordering":false,"tableFilters":[{"soil_type":[{"value":null},{"s":"arr"}]},{"s":"arr"}],"tableGrouping":null,"tableGroupingDirection":null,"tableSearch":"","tableSortColumn":null,"tableSortDirection":null,"activeTab":null,"mountedActions":[[],{"s":"arr"}],"mountedActionsArguments":[[],{"s":"arr"}],"mountedActionsData":[[],{"s":"arr"}],"defaultAction":[[],{"s":"arr"}],"defaultActionArguments":[[],{"s":"arr"}],"componentFileAttachments":[[],{"s":"arr"}],"areFormStateUpdateHooksDisabledForTesting":false,"mountedFormComponentActions":[[],{"s":"arr"}],"mountedFormComponentActionsArguments":[[],{"s":"arr"}],"mountedFormComponentActionsData":[[],{"s":"arr"}],"mountedFormComponentActionsComponents":[[],{"s":"arr"}],"mountedInfolistActions":[[],{"s":"arr"}],"mountedInfolistActionsData":[[],{"s":"arr"}],"mountedInfolistActionsComponent":null,"mountedInfolistActionsInfolist":null,"isTableLoaded":false,"tableRecordsPerPage":10,"tableColumnSearches":[[],{"s":"arr"}],"toggledTableColumns":[{"sample_id":true,"farm_name":true},{"s":"arr"}],"mountedTableActions":[["edit"],{"s":"arr"}],"mountedTableActionsData":[[[{"id":3,"sample_id":"9-18-02-26-02","farmer_id":9,"farm_id":9,"farm_name":"Quizon Coffee Farm","soil_type":"Clay","analysis_type":"with_lab","crop_variety":"Arabica","date_collected":"2026-02-18 00:00:00","location":"7.56241, 126.13294","ref_no":"ref1","submitted_by":"kwntoi","date_submitted":"2026-02-18 00:00:00","date_analyzed":"2026-02-18 00:00:00","lab_no":"lab1","field_no":"fieled1","ph_level":5.8,"nitrogen":0.15,"phosphorus":13,"potassium":150,"organic_matter":1.5,"recommendation":"SOIL ANALYSIS RECOMMENDATION REPORT
Source: Bureau of Soils & Water Management (BSWM) / FAO Soil Interpretation Guidelines
==========================================================================================
Crop     : Arabica
Soil Type: Clay
==========================================================================================

1. SOIL CONDITION ASSESSMENT
+----------------------+------------+-----------+------------------------------------------------------+
| Parameter            | Value      | Rating    | Remarks                                              |
+----------------------+------------+-----------+------------------------------------------------------+
| Soil pH              | 5.8        | Medium    | Good range for coffee (5.5-6.5). Maintain.           |
| Organic Matter       | 1.5%       | Low       | Low. Apply 3-5 t/ha compost annually.                |
| Nitrogen (N)         | 0.15%      | Low       | Deficient. Apply 60-90 kg N/ha in 2 splits.          |
| Phosphorus (P)       | 13 ppm     | Medium    | Adequate. Maintenance dose 20-40 kg P2O5/ha.         |
| Potassium (K)        | 150 ppm    | Medium    | Adequate. Maintenance dose 20-40 kg K2O/ha.          |
+----------------------+------------+-----------+------------------------------------------------------+

2. FERTILIZER RECOMMENDATION
   (Note: full application timing is in Section 4 below)
+-----------------+--------------------------------------+--------------------+----------------------+
| Nutrient        | Fertilizer Product                   | Rate (per ha)      | When to Apply        |
+-----------------+--------------------------------------+--------------------+----------------------+
| Nitrogen (N)    | Urea (46-0-0) or Complete Fert. (14- | 60-90 kg N/ha      | Split 2x (see Sec. 4 |
| Phosphorus (P)  | Solophos (0-18-0)                    | 20-40 kg P2O5/ha   | At planting (maint.) |
| Potassium (K)   | Muriate of Potash (0-0-60)           | 20-40 kg K2O/ha    | At planting (maint.) |
+-----------------+--------------------------------------+--------------------+----------------------+

3. SOIL AMENDMENT
+------------------------+------------------------------------+--------------+--------------------------+
| Concern                | Product                            | Rate         | When to Apply            |
+------------------------+------------------------------------+--------------+--------------------------+
| Organic Matter (Build- | Compost or Organic Amendments      | 3-5 t/ha     | Annually, every season   |
+------------------------+------------------------------------+--------------+--------------------------+

4. APPLICATION SCHEDULE SUMMARY
+----------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| When                       | Action / What to Do                                                  |
+----------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Every planting season      | Apply compost or organic matter to build soil health.                |
| At planting                | Apply phosphorus fertilizer near root zone (banded).                 |
| At planting (basal)        | Apply 50% of nitrogen + 50% of potassium fertilizer.                 |
| 1-2 months after planting  | Apply remaining 50% nitrogen + remaining 50% potassium (top-dress).  |
| Every year                 | Apply organic matter (compost/manure) to maintain soil health.       |
| Every 6-12 months          | Re-test soil to evaluate progress and update fertilizer plan.        |
+----------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------+

5. GOOD FARMING PRACTICES
  * Mulch around coffee plants with dried leaves or rice straw (5-10 cm thick).
  * Keep crop residues on the field after harvest -- do NOT burn them.
  * Plant legume cover crops during fallow season to naturally add nitrogen.
  * Avoid deep plowing to protect soil structure and organic matter.
  * Never apply fertilizer on extremely dry or waterlogged soil.
  * Always wash hands after handling fertilizers and chemicals.

==========================================================================================
IMPORTANT REMINDERS
==========================================================================================
  ! ALWAYS correct soil pH FIRST -- fertilizers do not work on wrong pH.
  ! SPLIT nitrogen and potassium -- never apply the full dose at once.
  ! RE-TEST your soil every 6-12 months after major amendments.
  ! BANDED application of phosphorus near roots is more efficient than broadcasting.

databaseQueries.query: SELECT `permissions`.*, `model_has_permissions`.`model_id` as `pivot_model_id`, `model_has_permissions`.`permission_id` as `pivot_permission_id`, `model_has_permissions`.`model_type` as `pivot_model_type` FROM `permissions` inner join `model_has_permissions` on `permissions`.`id` = `model_has_permissions`.`permission_id` WHERE `model_has_permissions`.`model_id` in (1) and `model_has_permissions`.`model_type` = 'App\\Models\\User'

SELECT `roles`.*, `model_has_roles`.`model_id` as `pivot_model_id`, `model_has_roles`.`role_id` as `pivot_role_id`, `model_has_roles`.`model_type` as `pivot_model_type` FROM `roles` inner join `model_has_roles` on `roles`.`id` = `model_has_roles`.`role_id` WHERE `model_has_roles`.`model_id` in (1) and `model_has_roles`.`model_type` = 'App\\Models\\User'
SELECT * FROM `soil_analysis` ORDER BY `id` DESC LIMIT 10 offset 0
SELECT `farms`.`name`, `farms`.`id` FROM `farms` ORDER BY `farms`.`name` ASC LIMIT 50

SELECT * FROM `notifications` WHERE `notifications`.`notifiable_type` = 'App\\Models\\User' and `notifications`.`notifiable_id` = 1 and `notifications`.`notifiable_id` IS not NULL and json_unquote(json_extract(`data`, '$."format"')) = 'filament' ORDER BY `created_at` DESC LIMIT 51 offset 0

SELECT count(*) as aggregate FROM `notifications` WHERE `notifications`.`notifiable_type` = 'App\\Models\\User' and `notifications`.`notifiable_id` = 1 and `notifications`.`notifiable_id` IS not NULL and json_unquote(json_extract(`data`, '$."format"')) = 'filament' and `read_at` IS NULL
